ref: At first, Lot just stood there. But the Lord wanted to save him. So the angels took Lot, his wife, and his two daughters by the hand and led them out of the city.
text: Nakoso né ɔtwetwe ye bo ase, yiti sɔ mmienzua nyɔ ne sosɔle ye ne ɔye ne ye mma mmrasua nyɔ nemɔ sa, na bɛfale ye befili kuro ne anu, ofikyɛ AWURADE nwuni ye anwumvoin.
translated: When the Lord's day comes, let us gather together, both young and old, and let us come together in unity and love, and let us come together in the city and the village, in the church and in the house, and let us worship the Lord.

translated: Bebuale said, "We have been told that he is a great one! He has given us the opportunity to go and fetch our inheritance and to come back and build our own house for him."

text: Nakoso aleɛ dwole ne, ɔfale ɔwa brasua Lea brɛle Yakob, na ɔne ye lale.
translated: Then he went to him, and the woman who was his mother, Lea, said to Jacob, "My son."

ref: “What do you want?” he asked in return. “The ring on that cord around your neck,” was her reply. “I also want the special walking stick you have with you.” He gave them to her, they slept together, and she became pregnant.
text: Na Yuda koso bisale ye kyɛ, “Nzikyɛ yeɛ mva nzi wɔ berɛ awowaa ɔ?” Na obuale kyɛ, “Wɔ nzɔanoɛ pɛteaa, ɔne nyamaa bɔ osinze so ne, yeɛ pɔmaa bɔ otira wɔ ne.” Yiti Yuda fa mane ye. Ɔne ye lale, na ɔne ye nwonzɛle.
translated: Then Judas said to them, “What will you give me if I deliver him over to you?” And they paid him thirty pieces of silver. So Judas agreed and began looking for a good time to betray him.

translated: If God had not given us the strength to work, we would not have been able to do anything. God's strength is what makes us strong; it is the strength of the strong and the weak.

translated: Jacob said to his brother, “Please, let me go, I beg you.” And his brother said to him, “What is it that you want me to do for you?” And Jacob wrestled with him.

translated: Jacob, when he was old, his sons gathered around him, and he blessed them, saying, "I see a multitude of people, like the stars of heaven, and like the sand on the seashore, and I bless you all."

ref: The Lord God then asked the woman, “What have you done?” “The snake tricked me,” she answered, “and I ate some of that fruit.”
text: Na AWURADE Nyameɛ bisale braa ne kyɛ, “Nzikyɛ nikye yeɛ ayɔ ye?” Na braa ne buale kyɛ, “Ewoo ne yeɛ ɔlakalakale me maa nnili ɔ.”
translated: Then AWURADE God said to him, “What is good, what is evil?” And he said to him, “I do not know, I am not able to know.”

ref: The Lord said to Abram, “I brought you here from Ur in Chaldea, and I gave you this land.”
text: Na osele ye kyɛ, “Me ole AWURADE bɔ mvale wɔ mvili Yure bɔ ɔwɔ Kaldea mmale ɔ, kyɛ méfa aseɛ he mema wɔ maa ɔyɛ wɔ deɛ.”
translated: Then the king said, "I have heard of the God of the Jews, who is mighty and does wonders. I want to know if this God can save me from the lions."

ref: The Lord has been good to my master and has made him very rich. He has given him many sheep, goats, cattle, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of silver and gold, and many slaves.
text: AWURADE ayira me mene paa, na wayɛ sona titire. Wama ye mmoaen ne nandwie, ɛdwetɛ, sikaa kɔkorɛ, ngoaa ne afaneɛ mɔ, afupɔngɔ ne afunum mɔ.
translated: I have many riches, and I am very wealthy. I have gold and silver, diamonds, money, houses and lands, cars and many other things.
